For instance you can achieve this with SAP Single Sign On or SAP Cloud Identity Services.
See this link as an example: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SINGLE_SIGN-ON/ec58da89706946a6ac63e348c27f00a0/0623094809b5422184394e1498dbdbb0.html?version=2.0

I suggest you to reach out to your trusted SAP security experts for more information and to discuss your situation.

Hope it helps
Emanuele

entry.title: Re: 在执行付款策略后，形成清算的银行科目后，后续如果过过账到总账对账单中，或者说导入对账项目后，如何形成冲销清算科目
entry.content: 导入银行对账单行项目处理： 1.形成了客户KH0001应收账款61020.00元； 2.形成可供应商GYS0001付款策略中形成的银行清算科目11002020.00金额24408.00元 3.进入管理银行对账单APP后，进行相应的对账单行项目导入， 4.导入系统过账后，未能形成冲账； 5.系统未能形成自动冲账，冲销对应的应收账款或者银行清算科目，即借：银行存款 贷：应收账款或借：银行清算科目 贷：银行存款

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Learning best practices for file namin...
entry.content:
Hi Teresa,

We have defined a strict naming convention for items, content objects that work fine for us.

All objects have the same logic: ABC-1234

Each unit in our company has their own "three letter acronym": eg LGL for Legal department, MKT, SLS, HRD, CPL. 

Then each unit is free to set up a range for their courses. For the LGL courses, we use range 0000-0999 for International law, the above 1000 for each country law...

Content objects are the same as the ItemID with an appropriate suffix:

LGL-1001-FR-01  here is for a content object in French, and we simply increment the numbers if there are multiple content objects in the same item. And versions would be LGL-1001-FR-01.1.

Since the folder on iContent uses there content object name, here we are safe to avoid any overwrite. Within the folder, we don't impose anything in the naming convention. We are on SF LMS now since 8 years, and have never cleaned up our iContent server, something I think about it, and then I prefer thinking about any other problem I would actually be able to solve... 

And in the same logic, we use the ItemID for the curricula and assignments profiles, as except for the New Hire stuff, most curricula we assign contain only one item.

This whole logic works well for use as:

  • it is easy to retrieve any relationship between objects.
  • each training unit has their own course object names.

We use a single domain for all admins, and since our LMS admins usually succeed in using this naming convention, life is great !

HTH,

Kris

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter March 2025
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

Work Smarter, Sell Better

Want to help your sales team work smarter, not harder? In our latest blog, we talk about the right capabilities in SAP Sales Cloud that can make all the difference. Plus, you can dive deeper by accessing the e-learning in SAP Learning Hub.

Access and details

Deployment Readiness: SAP Datasphere & SAP Analytics Cloud

In Q1 and stretching into Q2 we are piloting a new Deployment Readiness Check service for both SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud. This is a unique opportunity to launch your systems with confidence.

Details of prerequisites outlined in the blog.

Explore the New SAP Business AI Page on SAP BTP Value Map

Did you know that we now have a dedicated page in the SAP BTP Value Map that focuses exclusively on SAP Business AI relevant to SAP BTP? Find a structured way to explore, learn, and implement AI-driven solutions and services within SAP Business Technology Platform.

Check it out here (value maps access required)

All You Need to Know About SAP Cloud ALM

Join the upcoming Expert-guided Implementation session (EGI) for all details that you need to know about the benefits and capabilities of SAP Cloud ALM. In this workshop, you can test the functionalities in your own environment. In March, we offer the EGI in multiple languages.

Register through this blog post

Learn to integrate HCM with SAP Cloud Identity Services in SAP SuccessFactors and Migrate to IAS/IPS

Enroll our new E-learning to understand the process of integrating HCM with SAP Cloud Identity Services in SAP SuccessFactors and learn how to migrate your production instance to Identity Authentication Service (IAS) /Identity Provisioning Service (IPS).

Full details in this blog post

Understanding Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Reducing implementation risks for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is possible with Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) from SAP Enterprise Support. Eager to know more? Check out our new e-learning explaining available CQCs and visit our Value Map for tailored insights into your cloud ERP journey.

Read the article

Watch the Replays of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series

From beginning of the year, the Cloud ERP product experts hosted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Missed the live sessions? No worries!

Watch the replays on demand–anywhere, anytime.

Customer Support: Explore SAP’s AI-Driven Support Solutions

AI plays a crucial role in Customer Support at SAP, with generative AI and large language models providing opportunities for predictive support, automated outbreak detection, and proactive issue resolution. AI also assists customers in creating tickets and support engineers in providing efficient and high-quality service.

Customer Support: Explore SAP’s AI-Driven Support Solutions (YouTube)

Get Nominated for the SAP Innovation Awards

The SAP Innovation Awards is seeking inspirational customer and partner stories that have successfully adopted SAP Cloud solutions to meet or exceed business goals, optimize IT resources, increase user enablement, and improve productivity or learning with the help of SAP Enterprise Support.

Visit the SAP Innovation Awards website for information on how you can enter

How Swisscom Supports the Customer Transformation With SAP Cloud ALM

As an SAP partner, Swisscom can seamlessly integrate their own content into SAP Cloud ALM, streamlining and accelerating the transformation process.

Watch the video (YouTube)

Proactive Customer Care: SAP's Blueprint for E-commerce Success

Stay ahead in retail peak season with proactive customer care! In this SAP News Center article, we dig into our Holiday Support Readiness Program and how AI-driven support solutions help businesses prepare for high shopping periods.

Check out the article

How to Get Started With SAP S/4HANA Finance

Are you about to start with SAP S/4HANA Finance? Then don’t miss our interactive virtual Mee-the-Expert live session on March 11 that helps you get on board.

Register through this blog post: Get started with SAP S/4HANA Finance

Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Finance – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about all phases and activities involved in Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA. We the next session will take place on March 5.

More in this blog post: EGI on Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Finance

Deep dive into SAP S/4HANA Finance Fundamentals

Join us in a multiday Expert-guided Implementation (EGI) training session to discover everything you need to know about delta capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Finance. We have two sessions in March on, 6 and 13.

entry.title: How to generate your SAP Datasphere documentation automatically
entry.content:
As in all projects, what is consuming and which is most often botched by consultants is the documentation.
And for good reason, happy to do development, we are often less happy in the newsroom.
The search for an automatic solution is often a hope rarely encountered

It is with this in mind that I share with you the premises of my documentation solution for SAP Datasphere Based on a model created in SAP Datasphere and a story in SAC, here is the result obtained in real time

 

Of course, there are many possibilities to extend the model to answer any question.

Features:
- Information about artifacts, whether it's a model, a view, a table
- Full lineage from an analytical model
- List of fields
- List of source tables on which the analytical model depends

Overview of the current model:
Based on the technical tables of the DB HANA, you can find an overview of the analytical model used to generate the documentation

 

Still to do:
- find the formulas,
- the information in the Business Purpose fields and other labels

entry.title: Re: How to link a Storage bin / Material with a Storage L...
entry.content:
Hello @BBQMaster68 

You can't set up storage location to storage location transfers in WM without customizing in the Stor. Location Control in Warehouse Mgmt node. Without the configuration WM doesn't recognize that a stock in the storage type 4 belongs to the storage location 2. That has to be customized. Otherwise, LQ01 won't work.

Of course you can still move stock from the storage type 3 to the storage type 4 but it will stay in the storage location 1.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP Developer Challenge March - SAP Community Escape House - AI 🤖🧠
entry.content:
The second SAP Developer Challenge of the year is now available! The Developer Advocates have created the SAP Community Escape House - a virtual escape house (consisting of multiple rooms) that you can play in your browser. Read on for all the details.

SAP Developer Challenges are all about having fun, trying new things, and learning along the way. You can also earn a badge in the SAP Community upon successful completion. For this year, all SAP Developer Challenges will happen in the SAP Community Escape House. For each challenge, we will unlock a new room in the house, and they all have a specific focus topic. Completing a challenge means successfully escaping the room by solving a series of coding-related tasks/questions. This month is all about AI, specifically the generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine.

The details of January's challenge (UI5) are here: link.
The details of February's challenge (Integration) are here: link.

 

How to participate

Visit the SAP Community Escape House and enter your numeric SAP Community user ID. If you don't know your user ID, go to your SAP Community profile. You can find the numeric ID at the end of the URL.

SAP Community Escape House - Enter your SAP Community ID


Once you see the SAP Community Escape House, select the AI room and start exploring it.

SAP Community Escape House - Available rooms

The user interface of an escape room includes the room itself as well as a terminal. You can click the objects in the room to receive tips. Like in a real (physical) escape room, you will have to try out a few things and explore the room until you receive helpful tips and, eventually, instructions. These tips and instructions are printed in the terminal next to the room. The terminal is also used to submit solutions to tasks. Read the instructions you receive carefully to understand how.

SAP Community Escape House - AI room

If you successfully escape the room (you will know when that happens), you will receive a badge in the SAP Community. We will assign the badges once the challenge closes.

The schedule

The AI room was unlocked on March 3rd and the challenge closes on March 31st, so you will have to escape the room until then to qualify for a badge. We will also inactivate all the service keys you need to access Generative AI Hub by the end of the challenge. The room will stay unlocked until the end of the year so you can still try it out with your own system. Unlike the previous year, this SAP Developer Challenge does not consist of weekly sub-challenges. The next room (next challenge) will be unlocked in April.

Good luck and happy escaping!

entry.title: Hands-on Tutorial: Creating an FAQ assistant as tool for a BTP AI agent
entry.content:
If you have a collection of FAQs that you want to be easily accessible for your business users, then an assistant that is powered by a Large Language model might be part of the answer.

This blog explains how to create such a backend (REST-API), that could directly feed into a graphical chatbot, or that can even be used as tool in a more comprehensive AI agent. In case this introduction sounds somehow familiar, then you might have read an earlier blog, which evolved into this tutorial. New in this blog is the use of SAP HANA Cloud's embedding engine for text. Also, a separate blog will explain how to bring this FAQ logic into an AI agent running on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

Table of contents

  • Background
  • Architecture and Process Flows
  • Prerequisites
  • The Frequently Asked Questions
  • Obtaining the "best" answer to a user request
  • Deployment as REST-API on Cloud Foundry

You find all code of this tutorial in this repository (Jupyter Notebooks + Cloud Foundry application).

Background

A collection of Frequently Asked Questions can be a great help to deal with common requests for specific information. However, the longer the list, the harder it can be to find the one piece of information one is looking for. Having to scroll through a long list can be tedious, and a simple text search might miss the one item you are looking for.

Hence an FAQ assistant can be very useful, especially if it can deal with user questions, that are phrased differently to the curated list of Questions and Answers.

Maybe you are rolling out new software to your users (S/4?) and want to help them along finding their feet in the new system through an FAQ assistant. Or you have a list of FAQs for any other purpose, whether intended for internal colleagues or external contacts such as customers. A digital assistant that leverages the list of FAQs could help the users along.

In this blog you see how the backbone of such a digital assistant can be created, based on a fairly short list of FAQs about SAP and working at SAP. Whilst this example is quite simplified, the same overall approach has been working very well for a customer with a list of 200+ FAQs. Maybe you find some inspiration in this blog for your own project.

Architecture and Process Flows

Before looking at any code, let's first get an understanding of how the application works at high level.

The architecture and process flows are based on the requirement, that the assistant should not hallucinate. As exciting as Large Language Models are, when producing text they are producing text that seems likely to them, but the text might be simply made up and incorrect.

To ensure that the chatbot can become a trusted advisor, hallucinations need to be avoided. We achieve this, by using the Large Language Model to understand the user's request, to find the predefined Question from the existing FAQ that best matches that request. It's then easy to find the corresponding pre-defined answer for that question. The only text generation happens now, when the pre-defined answer is reworded to be a natural response to the incoming request. This introduces some risk, that the re-wording might introduce a mistake. However, customers seem to prefer the natural wording of the response, accepting a (I like to think small) risk of producing factually incorrect output. If you prefer, you can drop this last step of course and return the exact response from the knowledge pool, thereby avoiding any risk of a hallucination.

The overall process flow for an incoming request is:

  1. An incoming request is received, ie "What is the meaning of the letters SAP".
  2. This text is vectorised by SAP HANA Cloud's vector engine. The same engine finds the most similar pre-defined questions from its known collection of FAQs.
  3. The original incoming request and the reduced list of candidate questions (as determined by the vector engine in the previous step) are sent via the Generative AI Hub to a Large Language Model, with the prompt to identify which question is the best match. The model returns the ID of the single, most relevant question.
  4. Now that the best matching question from the predefined list of FAQs is known, the predefined answer that belongs to the predefined question from the FAQ is retrieved from SAP HANA Cloud.
  5. The Large Language Model is used again, now to adjust the working of the pre-defined answer to the wording of the incoming request. This step helps create a more natural conversation flow.
  6. This adjusted answer is returned as response to the incoming request. 

 

Administrators can upload Questions and Answers with this simple process flow:

  1. Upload the new Question and Answer to SAP HANA Cloud
  2. SAP HANA Cloud automatically creates the embedding of the question and stores the vector in the same table that holds the actual texts. We will see later how this is done.

Questions and Answers are stored in two separate tables. This allows for 1:n relationships between Answers and Question. This means, for each predefined Answer 1 or more Questions can be associated. This will be useful when improving and adjusting the assistant to different terminology from the different users. After all, there are many ways to phrase the same question.

The overall underlying Architecture is:

Since SAP HANA Cloud's embedding engine and the SAP-hosted mistral-large-instruct model are used, no externally hosted model needs to be called. All processing is happening on the BTP.

Prerequisites

To follow the implementation hands-on, you require these components:

So to follow the implementation hands-on, you require these components:

  • SAP Generative AI Hub with the model "mistral-large-instruct" deployed,
  • SAP HANA Cloud
    - to store the questions and answers
    - to create the embeddings of the questions with the vector engine
    - to compare questions with the vector engine
  • Python environment, ie Jupyter Notebooks in Miniconda,  for sandboxing and testing
  • Cloud Foundry on BTP if you want to deploy the application as REST-API

This blog assumes that you already have some familiarity with Python and Jupyter Notebooks. However, this project could also be a starting point to become familiar with those components. Personally, I like Miniconda to create a local Python environment and local Jupyter Notebooks. The blog "Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud" that was co-written with @YannickSchaper gives an overview of how to get started with our Python package hana_ml. That hana_ml package allows Data Scientists to work from Python with data that remains in SAP HANA Cloud (or SAP Datasphere). It can even trigger Machine Learning in SAP HANA Cloud, but we will use it here mostly to upload data, enrich the data and to trigger the vector engine.

The Frequently Asked Questions

We will use a fairly short list of FAQs as basis for the Chatbot. These are just a few examples taken from the FAQs about SAP overall (ie history and sustainability) and Jobs @ SAP. Kudos to who knows by heart what the abbreviation "SAP" actually stand for...  For all others there is the FAQ and our little custom assistant we are building.

The Questions and Answers for our chatbot are saved in two separate Excel files. This allows for specifying multiple Questions that belong to the same single Answer. Remember, all files and code used in this blog can be downloaded from this repository.  The code to upload the FAQs is in 010 Upload Questions and Answers.ipynb. To run the code yourself you should just have to enter your own logon credentials for SAP HANA Cloud and the Generative AI Hub into the file credentials.json. In this blog itself I am only showing the most important pieces of code. Please check the above repository to get the full code. To be able to execute the Notebooks you also need to install these Python packages

  • !pip install hana_ml
  • !pip install openpyxl
  • !pip install "generative-ai-hub-sdk[all]"

Uploading the data to SAP HANA Cloud is then easy with the hana_ml Python package. Establish a connection from Python to SAP HANA Cloud.

 

import hana_ml.dataframe as dataframe
conn = dataframe.ConnectionContext(
                                   address  = SAP_HANA_CLOUD_ADDRESS,
                                   port     = SAP_HANA_CLOUD_PORT,
                                   user     = SAP_HANA_CLOUD_USER,
                                   password = SAP_HANA_CLOUD_PASSWORD, 
                                  )
conn.connection.isconnected()

 

Load the questions from Excel (FAQ_ASSISTANT_ANSWERS.xlsx) for into a Pandas DataFrame.

 

#!pip install openpyxl
import pandas as pd
df_data= pd.read_excel ('FAQ_ASSISTANT_QUESTIONS.xlsx') 
df_data.head(5)

 

And upload that Pandas DataFrame to SAP HANA Cloud. The table FAQ_ASSISTANT_QUESTIONS will be created automatically. Now add to that new table a vector column, that will be automatically populated with the text embeddings of the QUESTION column.

 

dbapi_cursor = conn.connection.cursor()
dbapi_cursor.execute("""
ALTER TABLE FAQ_ASSISTANT_QUESTIONS
ADD (QUESTION_VECTOR REAL_VECTOR GENERATED ALWAYS AS VECTOR_EMBEDDING(QUESTION, 'QUERY', 'SAP_NEB.20240715'));
""")

 

Verify that SAP HANA Cloud's embedding engine has created the vectors as expected. 

 

conn.table('FAQ_ASSISTANT_QUESTIONS').head(5).collect()

 

You can now add additional rows to the table as usual and SAP HANA Cloud's embedding engine will automatically kick in to fill the vector column. The Notebook 010 Upload Questions and Answers.ipynb has an example that shows this in action.

Similarly, upload the pre-defined answers from FAQ_ASSISTANT_ANSWERS.xlsx. These texts do not need to be vectorised, as we are only comparing the questions with each other.

 

df_data = pd.read_excel ('FAQ_ASSISTANT_ANSWERS.xlsx') 
df_remote = dataframe.create_dataframe_from_pandas(connection_context=conn, 
                                                   pandas_df=df_data, 
                                                   table_name='FAQ_ASSISTANT_ANSWERS',
                                                   force=True,
                                                   replace=False)

 

Obtaining the "best" answer to a user request

Now let's play through a scenario of a user asking a question, which the application is trying to answer. This section's code is also in 020 Ask a Question.ipynb. Take a first question and have SAP HANA Cloud vectorise it.

 

user_question = 'What is the meaning of the letters SAP?'
sql = f'''SELECT VECTOR_EMBEDDING('{user_question}', 'QUERY', 'SAP_NEB.20240715') EMBEDDEDQUESTION FROM DUMMY;'''
user_question_embedding_str = conn.sql(sql).head(1).collect().iloc[0, 0]
user_question_embedding_str[:10]

 

This vectorisation is required so that the user question can be compared with the existing vectors of the known questions. This finds the most similar known questions.

 

sql = f'''SELECT TOP 200 "AID", "QID", "QUESTION", COSINE_SIMILARITY("QUESTION_VECTOR", TO_REAL_VECTOR('{user_question_embedding_str}')) AS SIMILARITY
        FROM FAQ_ASSISTANT_QUESTIONS ORDER BY "SIMILARITY" DESC, "AID", "QID" '''
df_remote = conn.sql(sql)
df_remote.head(20).collect()

 

Take the most promising questions and format that collection into a text element, which will become part of the prompt that is sent to the Large Language  Model to select the best match. We will see that each question is preceded by an ID, which is a combination of the QID and AID. This new composite ID will help us to retrieve the corresponding data from the tables in SAP HANA Cloud.

 

top_n = max(df_remote.filter('SIMILARITY > 0.95').count(), 10)
df_data = df_remote.head(top_n).select('AID', 'QID', 'QUESTION').collect()
df_data['ROWID'] = df_data['AID'].astype(str) + '-' + df_data['QID'].astype(str) + ': '
df_data = df_data[['ROWID', 'QUESTION']]
candiates_str = df_data.to_string(header=False,
                                  index=False,
                                  index_names=False)

 

Leverage that information in the prompt for the Large Language Model, by describing the task.

 

llm_prompt = f'''
Task: which of the following candidate questions is closest to this one?
{user_question}
Only return the ID of the selected question, not the question itself. The ID are the first values in each row. 
In case none of the candidate questions is a good match, return only: NONE

-----------------------------------

Candidate questions. Each question starts with the ID, followed by a :, followed by the question
{candiates_str}
'''
print(llm_prompt)

 

Specify which Large Language Model should be used on the Generative AI Hub (this model needs to be deployed there) and send the prompt off.

 

AI_CORE_MODEL_NAME  = 'mistralai--mistral-large-instruct'
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.native.openai import chat
messages = [{"role": "user", "content": llm_prompt}
           ]
kwargs = dict(model_name=AI_CORE_MODEL_NAME, messages=messages, temperature=0)
response = chat.completions.create(**kwargs)
llm_response = response.choices[0].message.content.lstrip()
llm_response

 

The model responds '1001-1', which refers to the first Question 1 of Answer 1001. And indeed, that's the correct match. Now get the answer to that question from SAP HANA Cloud.

 

aid = qid = None
matching_question = matching_answer = None

if llm_response != 'NONE':
   aid, qid = llm_response.split('-')

   # From HANA Cloud get the question from the FAQ that matches the user request best
   df_remote = conn.table('FAQ_ASSISTANT_QUESTIONS').filter(f''' "AID" = '{aid}' AND "QID" = '{qid}' ''').select('QUESTION')
   matching_question = df_remote.head(5).collect().iloc[0,0]
    
   # From HANA Cloud get the predefined answer of the above question from the FAQ
   df_remote = conn.table('FAQ_ASSISTANT_ANSWERS').filter(f''' "AID" = '{aid}' ''').select('ANSWER')
   matching_answer = df_remote.head(5).collect().iloc[0,0]
matching_answer

 

The mystery of what the letters SAP stand for has been solved. Now, optionally, you can tweak this pre-defined answer to be a more natural response to the initial incoming question. Let's ask the Large Language Model again.

 

llm_prompt = f'''
Task: Answer the following question. Only consider the context that is provided further below.
{user_question}

-----------------------------------

Context to consider to answer the above question:
{matching_answer}
'''
messages = [{"role": "user", "content": llm_prompt}           ]
kwargs = dict(model_name=AI_CORE_MODEL_NAME, messages=messages, temperature=0)
response = chat.completions.create(**kwargs)
natural_answer = response.choices[0].message.content.lstrip()
print(f"""
The user question was:
{user_question}

The selected question from the FAQ is:
{matching_question}

The pre-defined answer to the selected question is:
{matching_answer}

The natural answer is:
{natural_answer}""")

 

Indeed, the natural answer picks of some of the initial question's terminology and feels like a more natural response.

 

Deployment as REST-API on Cloud Foundry

Above we have the application logic. Now we want to make it available as REST-API to a custom BTP AI Agent. That AI agent will be described in a separate Tutorial that will hopefully be published soon...

For now we create the REST-API on Cloud Foundry. In case you are new to Cloud Foundry, then Steps 1 and 2 of this Learning Journey get you started. The application that we deploy here consists of 4 files. The full code is available in this repository folder.  

  1. faq.py ➔ holds the actual application logic
  2. manifest.yaml ➔ contains for instance the memory requirements and environment variables
  3. requirements.txt ➔ lists the Python packages that will be installed
  4. runtime.txt ➔ specifies the Python version (python-3.12.x)

Before pushing the application to Cloud Foundry you need to make the following adjustments

  1. In faq.py specify your SAP HANA Cloud logon credentials. For productive use you may want to store these more securely, ie in the SAP Credential Store. An example is in this blog. 
  2. In manifest.yml enter your credentials for the SAP Generative AI Hub.

Then push your application to Cloud Foundry.

 

cf push

 

And you should see the URL of your REST-API!

 

Test the URL from your preferred environment. This could just be the Python notebook.

 

import requests
paylod = {'user_request': 'Who is the boss at SAP?'}
url = 'https://REPLACEWITHYOURURL'
headers = {'Accept' : 'application/json', 'Content-Type' : 'application/json'}
r = requests.get(url, json=paylod, headers=headers, verify=False)
response = r.json()
faq_response = response['faq_response'] # final answer
faq_response_log = response['faq_response_log'] # log of the individual steps

 

And it worked! We get the official answer as written on SAP's own FAQ, adjusted to a more natural conversation flow.

Now you might think, wouldn't it be nice if the assistant was getting the ultimate answer (the name of the CEO) from the answer's link? That's where the BTP AI agent comes in. Just bear with me, this will be described in a separate blog!

entry.title: 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之加值中心集成 （台湾地区本地化需求）
entry.content:
本文介绍的台湾特有的业务场景:通过加值中心集成申报VAT。这是客户系统实施嵌入式分析的一次概念验证的成果，以验证可以在SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition中获取到台湾VAT申报所需要集成的相关电子发票信息，并支持将其数据可视化。本文以台湾某加值中心的集成需求为例，向读者介绍如何借助嵌入式分析获取到相关业务数据，支持客户进行集成。

 

客户的需求

 

台湾地区客户可通过自选的加值中心进行增值税申报。在系统导入专案的实施过程中，其面临的主要挑战是抽取并整合来自不同交易凭证的源数据。由于各个加值中心在格式和字段标准上存在差异，目前SAP并未提供针对这些情况的开箱即用集成解决方案。因此，客户需要充分利用现有的CDS视图，通过定制开发，获取所需的数据，并满足特定格式要求，实现数据的高效集成。

 

鉴于上述挑战，客户可考虑采用嵌入式分析功能来满足该业务需求。

本文将基于以下VAT申报集成字段需求示例展开：

元素順序

元素名稱

最大長度

必要性

1

主檔代號

3

M

2

發票號碼

10

M

3

發票日期

8

M

4

發票時間

6

M

5

發票傳送日期

8

O

6

發票傳送時間

6

O

7

發票接收日期

8

O

8

發票接收時間

8

O

9

發票金額（未稅）

12

M

10

稅率別

1

M

11

營業稅額

12

M

12

發票人統一編號

8

M

13

受票人統一編號

8

M

14

發票人名稱

60

M

15

發票人名稱二

20

O

16

發票人地址

75

M

17

發票負責人

10

O

18

發票人電話

15

M

19

受票人名稱

60

M

20

受票人地址

75

O

21

稅捐稽徵處名稱

40

O

22

核准日

8

O

23

核准文

40

O

24

核准號

20

O

25

款項別

1

M

26

相關號碼

20

O

27

彙開標記

1

O

 

 

解决方案

主要步骤：

  • 分析业务需求，确立S/4HANA Cloud中对应的资料源CDS及字段名
  • 针对尚未Release的标准CDS，创建自定义业务对象
  • 创建自定义CDS视图
  • 创建自定义分析查询
  • 创建多维报表

具体步骤将在下面详细描述。为了保护客户敏感数据，一些截图来自虚拟测试系统。

  1. 分析业务需求，确立S/4HANA Cloud中对应的资料源CDS及字段名

当前S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition暂未提供整合的电子发票CDS视图，故专案实施需要通过各个模组的凭证资料源CDS来获取相关数据，并加工整合。

  • 以前文提到的业务需求为例，识别出以下核心标准CDS视图作为资料来源：
  • CDS：I_BillingDocument、I_BillingDocumentItem

覆盖资料：基于请款凭证的发票抬头及行明细信息

  • CDS：I_Address_2

覆盖资料：发票人与受票人的名称、电话、地址等信息

  • CDS：I_Businesspartnertaxnumber

覆盖资料：受票人的统一编号信息

  • CDS：I_KR_BusinessPlace

覆盖资料：发票人的营业场所详细信息

  • 需要特殊处理的数据
    • 税率别

依照请款凭证行项目的税率分为：1 应税、2 零税率、3 免税。从系统中的税码到税分类的转换，需要在 CDS 数据加工中处理，详见步骤3。

  • 营业场所与销售办公室的关系

发票人的营业场所信息（例如发票人统一编号、印章电话等）需要通过请款凭证行项目的Sales Office字段读取其对应的Business Place 信息。但当前系统中暂无 Sales Office 与 Business Place 关联关系的 CDS，故此关系需要借助自定义业务对象（Custom Business Object）来维护实现，详见步骤2。

           

除本文提供的实施建议外，若您期望SAP能通过标准产品内容提供统整的台湾电子发票CDS视图，欢迎您通过贵司的 S-User访问https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/340825，投出您宝贵的一票。

 

  1. 针对尚未Release的标准CDS，创建自定义业务对象

 

由于目前没有现成的CDS视图展示Business Place和Sales Office的关联关系，可以创建一个自定义业务对象YY1_BUSINESSPLACE2SALESOFF。该对象将保存Business Place和Sales Office之间的对应关系，披露相关数据。

 

 

本文假定业务场景为，台湾地区设台北、高雄各一个营业场所。在自定义业务对象中维护其对应关系如下。

注意该资料信息应与配置点 103391 Assign Business Places to Sales Offices 保持一致。

 

 

 

  1. 创建自定义CDS视图

然后需要创建2个CDS披露相关数据源。找到应用自定义 CDS 视图并创建新的 CDS 视图。

首先创建的是标准CDS 视图YY1_BillingItemTW。该CDS视图主要披露了电子发票的详细信息，例如发票代码，金额，发票人和受票人等信息。选择I_BillingDocumentItem作为主数据源，添加I_BillingDocument, I_Businesspartnertaxnumber以及自定义业务对象YY1_BUSINESSPLACE2SALESOFF。

 

添加参数起始日期和截止日期。

 

添加主数据源和关联数据源里的元素，例如发票代码，税额，金额等。

 

添加必要的计算，例如稅率別。由于I_TW_TaxCode为开放用于开发，所以需要通过创建计算，按固定逻辑获取配置级别的税码分类。本例中的计算逻辑如下：

A1/A2/A6/A9: 1

A3/A5/A7/AW: 2

A8/AZ: 3

 

维护元素属性并设置过滤。

 

保存并发布该视图。

然后基于创建成功的CDS视图YY1_BillingItemTW，创建第二个CDS视图YY1_BillingCubeTW，并添加关联数据源I_KR_BusinessPlace和I_Address_2，主要用于披露受票人和发票人的详细信息。该CDS视图为cube类型。

 

添加必要的元素以及计算。例如受票人和发票人地址。通过创建计算，组合地址信息，以满足不同加值中心的格式需求。

 

 

维护元素属性，然后保存并发布 CDS 视图。到目前为止，所有视图已创建完成。

  1. 创建自定义分析查询

在主页中找到“自定义分析查询和服务”应用并创建新的自定义查询。（⚠️自定义分析查询和服务是新的创建分析查询的应用程序，后续支持发布为Ina Service，用于后续MDA报表，审核手册和eSAC 故事。）

 

选择YY1_BillingCubeTW作为数据源并添加字段。

 

 

然后保存并发布查询。

接下来需要将创建好的查询发布为InA服务。进入管理InA服务。

 

添加查询YY1_BILLINGTW，然后发布。



 

 

  1. 创建多维报表

进入管理KPI和报表应用，选择多维报表，点击创建，添加创建好的查询为数据源。保存并发布。

 

根据业务需要，为该报表维护业务目录。多维报表创建完成。客户可以根据自己的业务需要，调整页面布局和格式，增加显示一些关键度量的图表可视化。

 

 

结论

通过使用嵌入式分析，客户可以高效地利用其强大的分析功能来解决业务上分析问题。当然客户也可以直接将CDS封装为API进行消费。具体操作可参考链接。

在未来，我们将继续与读者分享更多有关嵌入式分析的成功案例。

有关SAP S/4HANA Cloud嵌入式SAC的更多信息，可以查看以下链接：

  • S/4HANA Cloud嵌入式分析——数据展现的灵活扩展：click here
  • 嵌入式分析云客户用例：如何在S/4HANA Cloud中创建定制SAC story和dashboard: click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之生产成本分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之货物移动分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之库龄分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之物料库存分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之销售毛利分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之外向交货和开票概览：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之交货完成分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之进销存报表：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之集团报表：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之期间费用表（跨三年比较）：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之财务关键指标比较：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之采购对账仪表盘：click here
  • 嵌入式分析客户用例分享之AVC： click here

entry.title: Integration Flow reuse Javascript functions across Script Blocks
entry.content:
I have several javascript blocks in my iFlow and want to share some functions between them, as they are used on all of them. To achieve that, I created a javascript file like below and uploaded it to the local references of the iFLow as shared_function_file.js.

 

export function shared_function(variable) {
  return "cool";
}

In the script, where I want to use this function, I tried to import it with

import { shared_function} from "shared_function_file.js";

which results in the error:

javax.script.ScriptException: identifier is a reserved word: import

Is it somehow possible to share functions between script blocks and if yes, how it is working?

entry.title: Re: ADT Feature Availability Matrix for AS ABAP Releases
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" With the ABAP development tools for eclipse we follow the strategy that the latest eclipse client is compatible with all ABAP backends with version 7.31 SP4 and higher. Most of the basic features in the area of source code editing are available against all these backend versions. But as some of the features are dependent from backend functionality it is clear that not all features are available against every backend system version. "

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: Payment Method not viewable in APP Manage Supplier Line
entry.content: Hi SFerrand,

entry.title: Re: Balancing field 'Profit Center' is not filled in line...
entry.content:
Hello,

To address this issue, you can consider the following steps:

1. **Assign a Profit Center**: Before proceeding with the goods issue, you can assign a profit center to the material. You can do this by updating the master data of the material or by adjusting the relevant stock account assignments to include a profit center.

2. **Check System Messages Configuration**: Use the 'Manage Your Solution' app to check whether the message related to the profit center is set as an Error ('E'), Warning ('W'), or if it can be suppressed. The specific message you might want to look for in the configuration item '103090 System Messages' is related to profit center assignment errors or warnings.

3. **Alternative Posting Options**: If it is difficult to assign a profit center directly, another option could be to make an adjustment entry by using a different movement type or accounting measure that allows you to bypass this validation temporarily, ensuring all necessary adjustments are made swiftly after.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Payment Method not viewable in APP Manage Supplier Line
entry.content:
Hi SFerrand,

the “Manage Supplier Line Items” app shows the payment method which is entered in the line item directly.
Usually, there is no payment method entered in the supplier line item and the payment is automatically determined during the payment run. By leaving the payment method in the line item empty, you allow the system to determine the best payment method based on your configuration.
Entering a payment method for an open line item, however, would restrict the available payment method for this line item to this very single payment method.

You have to navigate into the Manage Business Partner Master Data app to see which payment methods are entered.

Best wishes
Christina

entry.title: Re: service PO or a normal PO for a subcontracting in SAP...
entry.content:
For the Operation Subcontracting usually should maintain the specified “Control Key （ex. PP02）”, and it will create a PR automatically when the production order created. And then you can transfer the PR to PO, and the price in PO will record the subcontracting cost, the cost will be recorded into the production order when did GR of PO.

According to your question, “Please suggest, shall we go with a service Po or a normal PO? ", I think you can just use the normal PO.

BTW, you can also create a material to do the subcontracting process, you can set "F 30" for the material and do the following purchasing subcontracting process.

entry.title: Re: SAP Intelligence - Delete processes, but that the pip...
entry.content:
I would like to reduce the number of our cases in SAP Intelligence. However, I would like to delete the processes, but in such a way that the pipelines continue to exist.
To do this, I wanted to select the processes to be deleted and delete the process under Settings. I seem to have two options here. Which option would be correct to reduce the number of cases but keep the pipelines? Is there a possibility?

 

Hello, as you want to reduce the number of cases in the Process Analysis layer, but keep the remaining pipelines in the Data Management layer, my suggestion would be the following:

  1. Inside of the Business Objects SQL scripts in Process Data Management, I would recommend to add a SQL filter that none of the business objects would fulfil. 
    1. E.g. a WHERE statement at the end of the Case Attributes script that says " WHERE c_caseid = '123' ", or some combination that is similar to this.
    2. Note: If c_caseid is not yet materialized, you may need to reference other fields that are used to make up the case id column.
  2. Run the T&L
    1. If successful, your process in the Process Analysis layer should reveal 0 cases.

If you follow this approach, this would also ensure that your dashboards and other process analysis configurations remain in tact, whilst you can modify the SQL scripts in Process Data Management to bring back more cases incrementally.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM maintenance times
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to provide you with some information regarding the release cycle of SAP Cloud ALM.

The platform follows a bi-weekly release cycle to make new features visible to customers. This process is also known as feature activation. It's important to note that new code and features are deployed into production daily. However, these new features are deactivated by using feature toggles until they are finally released to customers in one of the bi-weekly feature releases. The deployment process is designed to ensure zero downtime and feature activation are enabled via organizational and technical means. These concepts ensure that no user session or API call is impacted by these changes. As a result, users will not notice the updates at all. For more detailed information, you can refer to this link: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm#discover_task-additional_information

Best regards
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Bonus Calculation Explanation - Variable Pay
entry.content:
Hi Olga,

I cannot access the attachment so apologies if some details are provided there. 

First of all, I am a bit confused by the formula you mention (base + Individual), as I dont think it exists. The only formulas that are available are all "base multiplied by something". If you use an additive formula, then it would be "base*(business + individual)". If you do not use business goals, then it will result in "base*individual". 

The base is determined in the calculation settings and is usually the salary * target percentage for the bonus, but I do not know how it is configured for you. 

If you wanted to achieve something like (base + base*individual), then you would have to set up the calculation to be "base*(business + individual)", with a single business goal set with a result of 100%. 

Without more details I am unable to help you further I am afraid

entry.title: Re: Different results between iRPA and Build Process Auto...
entry.content:
Obviously, I can only see 100 pages because of limits imposed by SBPA: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/quotas-restrictions-and-limits#process-workflow-capability

For the output mapping, the limit per task is 100, which compels me to customize my project based on these limits. This is a drawback compared to iRPA.

For those who would face the same issue: if you want this to have a chance to be improved for your further usage, please raise a request as suggested here 0000000011 so SAP dev team considers to implement this in the future.

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention IWS "Transition to SAP S/4HANA Using Downtime-Optimized Conversion"(March 19, 2025)
entry.content:
Virtual

Agenda

March 19, 2025

Dear Valued Customer,

We are pleased to invite you to the SAP MaxAttention Innovation Workshop focusing on Transition to SAP S/4HANA Using Downtime-Optimized Conversion taking place virtually on March 19th, 2025. Please find the current agenda here.

In this workshop you’ll understand various aspects of Downtime-Optimized Conversion to minimize the business downtime while transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition or On-Premise or Hyperscalers.

  • Tools and Approaches to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition or On-Premise, focusing on the Downtime-Optimized Conversion to minimize your business downtime during the transition
  • Data Transition Validation during the Transition to SAP S/4HANA to ensure data consistency as a foundation to better use the SAP S/4HANA
  • How SAP MaxAttention will be able to plan and safeguard your Downtime-Optimized Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Project

You will be able to grasp a good understanding of how to plan your Downtime-Optimized Conversion project to secure minimal business disruption. SAP MaxAttention will be able to support you in every step of your journey of transitioning to SAP S/4HANA to minimize the risk and maximize the result!

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Important

Please note that the 'Attend' button which can be found within this blog post does not serve as a formal registration for the event.

To officially register, please reach out to me, paul.schropp@sap.com and I will ensure that your registration is properly processed.

We look forward to having you join us for our upcoming events and thank you for your understanding!

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere, SAP BW bridge: Demystifying the Remote Conversion
entry.content:
Hi KP,

1) Calc. views are not supported in BW bridge and need to be recreated in Datasphere.

2) Query objects are not functional in BW bridge, but you can use the Entity Import to move the elements to Datasphere to reuse the semantics and build your data model, which can be consumed by SAC. Please see:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/entity-import-model-transfer-for-single-query-sap-bw-bridge/ba-p/13557138 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/c8a54ee704e94e15926551293243fd1d/a3d4a2f91bea4810ba8839ff73577dac.html?locale=en-US

Kind regards, Felipe

entry.title: Re: see 0F document type data in reconciliation balance a...
entry.content:
Hi,

we encountered a similar issue after upgrading to S4H2023.

Since S4H 2022 ICMR (Intercompany Matching and Reconciliation) uses the FYV (Fiscal Year variant) assigned to the relevant consolidation version. In out case we discovered that that no FYV is assigned to version Y10. If no FYV is specified, ICMR cannot read data from ACDOCA, which leads to the missing data.

2935283 - Intercompany Reconciliation Report Can't Read Data from ACDOCA - SAP for Me

You assign the FYV in SPRO here:

I hope this helps : )
